Title: A Synthetic Facultative CAM-Like Shuttle in C3 Rice Plants

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtALMT9 was introduced as the malate transporter module in CBP rice. 2. It localized to the vacuolar membrane. 3. Together with CAM enzymes, it supported nocturnal malate accumulation and reduced vacuolar pH in rice leaves.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expressed under OsActin1 promoter in the final construct; facilitates vacuolar malate storage rather than catalysis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vacuolar storage of malate enables nocturnal organic acid accumulation and CAM-like flux
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vacuolar malate transport/storage and contributes to lower vacuolar pH and CAM-like malate accumulation in rice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; subcellular localization; immunoblot; metabolite profiling; vacuolar pH measurement
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transporter module for engineering vacuolar organic acid storage in synthetic CAM designs; should be combined with flux-generating enzymes.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSLAC1 knockout was used as the stomatal regulation module/receptor background for CBP engineering. 2. slac1 mutants showed normal growth under normal conditions but rapid water loss and rolled leaves under drought. 3. In the CBP background, high stomatal conductance was associated with reduced intrinsic water use efficiency and poorer drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a metabolic enzyme; used to alter stomatal behavior in support of CAM-like engineering. Knockout line slac1-1 served as receptor for multigene transformation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates the CBP chassis with altered stomatal conductance but reduces water-use efficiency and drought performance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; drought assay; gas exchange analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought treatment; PEG treatment; diurnal gas exchange conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can improve photosynthetic efficiency under normal conditions as a chassis trait, but constitutive disruption is unsuitable for drought-prone deployment; inducible stomatal control is recommended.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Rapid water loss; rolled leaf phenotype under drought; lower iWUE; lower survival under drought stress

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nocturnal carboxylation module of CAM-like bypass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with other CAM module genes to engineer facultative CAM-like carbon sequestration in rice; native promoter use preserves nocturnal expression rhythm.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In CBP rice leaves, nocturnal expression peaking after about 2 h darkness and remaining high until morning
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in pineapple leaves; induced by darkness and circadian-regulated
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM)-like carbon fixation bypass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CA activity was not measured due to lack of method in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to nocturnal carbon fixation and supports nighttime malate accumulation in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoCA1 from pineapple was introduced into rice as part of the nocturnal carboxylation module. 2. It showed night-peaking expression in CBP rice similar to pineapple. 3. Together with other CBP genes, it supported CAM-like malate accumulation and improved photosynthetic performance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; qRT-PCR diurnal expression; immunoblot; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrates CO2 to bicarbonate for PEPC-mediated fixation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nocturnal carboxylation module of CAM-like bypass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key nocturnal carboxylation enzyme for synthetic CAM installation; useful in multigene engineering to enhance carbon fixation and yield.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in nocturnal CO2 fixation support, but not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression at night in CBP rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in pineapple leaves; induced by darkness and circadian-regulated
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM)-like carbon fixation bypass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PEPC activity in CBP lines was 51-61% higher than CK.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May contribute indirectly to increased respiratory flux when bypass drives TCA cycle
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nocturnal oxaloacetate and downstream malate accumulation in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoPEPC1 was selected from pineapple due to high leaf expression and dark induction. 2. It showed nocturnal expression in CBP rice. 3. PEPC activity increased markedly and supported CAM-like malate accumulation and improved photosynthetic/carboxylation efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; immunoblot; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carboxylates phosphoenolpyruvate with bicarbonate to form oxaloacetate

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Malate formation and daytime decarboxylation-associated module of CAM-like bypass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful module for promoting malate pool establishment in engineered CAM-like rice; may need coordinated tuning with TCA cycle to reduce respiratory loss.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression during the day in CBP rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and participates in daytime malate turnover
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM)-like carbon fixation bypass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MDH activity increased 14-30% in CBP lines; paper describes MDH in both malate formation and daytime metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced malate flux may feed TCA cycle and elevate respiration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports malate synthesis/turnover and contributes to CAM-like metabolic flux in rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoMDH1 was selected from pineapple and expressed well in CBP rice. 2. It localized to the cytoplasm and showed elevated enzyme activity. 3. It contributed to diurnal malate/OAA changes and altered TCA-cycle-linked carbon metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; immunoblot; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon sequestration intermediate supporting photosynthesis and TCA entry
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces oxaloacetate to malate in the CAM-like bypass

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Daytime decarboxylation module of CAM-like bypass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful daytime decarboxylation/regeneration component for CAM engineering; should be coordinated with malate transport and stomatal control.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression during the day in CBP rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Regenerates PEP and releases CO2 indirectly supporting Calvin cycle
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM)-like carbon fixation bypass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PEPCK activity was 13-32% higher in CBP lines.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May contribute to elevated respiration associated with active bypass metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports daytime decarboxylation and PEP regeneration, enhancing photosynthetic carbon assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoPEPCK1 was introduced as part of the daytime decarboxylation module. 2. It showed daytime-biased expression and cytoplasmic localization in rice. 3. Elevated enzyme activity accompanied higher PEP accumulation during daytime and improved carbon fixation traits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; immunoblot; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports daytime CO2 release for Rubisco and PEP regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts oxaloacetate to phosphoenolpyruvate with release of CO2 in the daytime module

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Daytime decarboxylation/regeneration module of CAM-like bypass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important chloroplast PEP regeneration gene for synthetic CAM/carbon sequestration engineering; suitable for gene stacking with PEPC and PEPCK.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression during the day in CBP rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool (PEP) for carboxylation cycle and supports Calvin-cycle-associated carbon flux
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crassulacean acid metabolism (CAM)-like carbon fixation bypass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PPDK activity was 9-16% higher in CBP lines.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: By increasing flux capacity it may be associated with higher respiration demand in CBP plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes daytime PEP regeneration and supports improved photosynthesis in CBP rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoPPDK1 was chosen from pineapple due to high leaf expression. 2. It localized to chloroplasts and showed daytime-biased expression in rice. 3. Increased PPDK activity coincided with higher daytime PEP and enhanced photosynthetic capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; immunoblot; subcellular localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports PEP regeneration and carbon assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts pyruvate to phosphoenolpyruvate in chloroplasts using ATP and phosphate transfer
